title: Piero Cipollone: The digital euro - preparing for a potential launch
summary: ECB Executive Board Member Piero Cipollone testified before the European Parliament's Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs on March 24, 2026, providing an update on the digital euro initiative, which euro area leaders have reaffirmed as vital for strengthening EU monetary sovereignty and reducing retail payment fragmentation. The Eurosystem is continuing technical preparations but will only issue the digital euro once enabling legislation is in place. Additionally, Pontes, the Eurosystem's DLT solution for central bank money settlement of DLT-based transactions, is slated for Q3 2026 launch.
key_points: Euro area leaders reaffirmed the vital importance of advancing the digital euro initiative as essential to strengthening EU monetary sovereignty
The digital euro aims to reduce fragmentation in retail payments and support the integrity and resilience of the Single Market
The Eurosystem will only issue the digital euro once the legislation is in place and in full compliance with that legislation
Pontes, the Eurosystem's DLT solution, will launch in Q3 2026 to enable central bank money settlement for DLT-based transactions
Earlier in March 2026, the ECB published a roadmap for Appia, an initiative to build an integrated European market for digital assets with private and public stakeholders
The views expressed are those of the speaker and do not necessarily reflect those of the BIS
why_it_matters: This testimony signals continued ECB commitment to the digital euro project and provides a timeline for wholesale DLT settlement infrastructure via Pontes. The distinction between the digital euro (retail CBDC) and wholesale market infrastructure (Pontes, Appia) clarifies the ECB's phased approach to digital currency and asset settlement. Financial market participants should monitor legislative progress and technical deployment timelines.
macro_context: The speech frames the digital euro within broader EU monetary sovereignty objectives and Single Market integrity. The reference to reducing retail payment fragmentation suggests concerns about fragmented payment markets within the euro area, which has macro implications for financial integration and banking sector competition.
market_structure_context: The speech discusses wholesale financial market infrastructure developments: Pontes (DLT solution launching Q3 2026) for central bank money settlement of DLT-based transactions, and Appia (published March 2026) for an integrated European digital assets market. These represent changes to market plumbing and settlement infrastructure.
